name: Reentrancy
description: External call before state update allows attacker to re-enter and drain funds
fix_description: Apply Checks-Effects-Interactions pattern: zero balance BEFORE external call, add reentrancy guard

name: Integer Overflow/Underflow
description: Arithmetic operations can overflow/underflow leading to incorrect balances
fix_description: Use SafeMath library or Solidity 0.8+ built-in overflow checks

name: Unchecked Call Return Value
description: Return value of low-level call is not checked, silently failing transfers
fix_description: Always check return value of .call() and revert on failure

name: Unprotected Ether Withdrawal
description: Anyone can withdraw funds without authorization check
fix_description: Add access control modifier (onlyOwner, onlyAdmin, etc.)

name: Unprotected Selfdestruct
description: Contract can be self-destructed by anyone, destroying all funds

name: State Variable Default Visibility
description: State variables without explicit visibility may behave unexpectedly
fix_description: Always specify visibility explicitly (private, internal, public)

name: Assert Violation
description: Using assert for input validation wastes gas and may cause DoS
fix_description: Use require() for input validation, not assert()

name: Delegatecall to Untrusted Callee
description: Delegatecall to user-controlled address can execute arbitrary code in context of contract
fix_description: Restrict delegatecall targets to whitelisted addresses, add access control

name: DoS with Failed Call
description: Loop over addresses where a single revert blocks all operations
fix_description: Use pull-over-push pattern or handle individual failures gracefully

name: Transaction Order Dependence
description: Using tx.origin for authentication allows phishing attacks
fix_description: Use msg.sender for authentication, never tx.origin

name: Authorization through tx.origin
description: tx.origin authentication can be exploited by phishing contracts
fix_description: Replace tx.origin with msg.sender for authorization

name: Block values as time proxies
description: block.timestamp can be manipulated by miners within a small range
fix_description: Accept small timestamp manipulation (15s range) for non-critical timing

name: Shadowing State Variables
description: Local variable shadows state variable, leading to wrong value used
fix_description: Rename variables to avoid shadowing, use distinct names

name: Weak Randomness
description: Using blockhash/timestamp for randomness is predictable by miners
fix_description: Use Chainlink VRF or commit-reveal scheme for secure randomness

name: Message call with external data
description: Arbitrary call with user-controlled data and target
fix_description: Restrict target addresses and add access control
